messages.say:
Yes. Several non-gate parts of the newPhasePlanProcess would materially benefit from being converted into reusable pattern programs using the same philosophy as the “Reusable Gates and Automation” document


NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_plan.schema…


.


Below is a straight assessment of where reuse will pay off the most, what the reusable pattern should be, and why it matters.



1. Phase Execution Itself (Beyond Gates)


Current state


Each phase embeds:


Custom execution flow

Phase-specific validation logic

Ad-hoc evidence handling

One-off retry / stop rules


This mirrors the pre-gate problem you already solved for validation.


Convert into a reusable pattern


Pattern: Deterministic Phase Runner (DPR)


Invariant flow (same for every phase):


Validate phase contract

Validate required inputs + schemas

Acquire declared write locks

Execute commands/templates

Validate outputs against schemas

Emit PhaseResult + HandoffEnvelope

Advance or halt (binary)


What varies (data-only):


Required inputs

Commands/templates

Produced artifacts

Advance rule

Write set


Why this matters


Eliminates “special phase logic”

Makes phases auditable, replayable, and swappable

Aligns phases with the exact same discipline as gates



2. Artifact Transport Between Phases


Current state


Artifacts are passed implicitly via paths and conventions:


Evidence scattered across directories

Phase inputs inferred, not declared

Provenance reconstructed after the fact


Convert into a reusable pattern


Pattern: Artifact Envelope + Handoff Contract


Every phase:


Consumes one envelope

Produces one envelope


Envelope contains:


Identity (run_id, plan_id, phase_id, attempt)

Artifact references (path + hash + schema_id)

Evidence references

Provenance (what produced what)

Snapshot signatures


Why this matters


Deterministic replay

Phase isolation

Zero ambiguity about “what the next phase sees”

Enables future distributed execution safely



3. Self-Healing Outside of Gates


Current state


Self-healing is mostly framed around gates, but:


Phase failures

Artifact mismatches

Write-policy violations
are handled inconsistently.


Convert into a reusable pattern


Pattern: Binary Step + Deterministic Remediation Loop
(same BG-DRL concept, applied beyond gates)


Apply it to:


Phase execution

Registry writes

Artifact promotion

Integration/merge steps


Invariant loop:


Detect failure

Normalize failure fingerprint

Classify failure_mode

Apply ordered heal templates

Re-run step

Escalate if unresolved


Why this matters


One healing model everywhere

Predictable retries

No bespoke “if this fails, do X” logic



4. Registry Writes and Mutations


Current state


Registry writes already follow rules, but:


Enforcement logic is spread

Evidence capture varies

Rollback semantics differ by context


Convert into a reusable pattern


Pattern: Controlled Registry Mutation Program


Invariant steps:


Validate write intent against write policy

Lock registry segment

Apply patch (RFC-6902 only)

Validate registry schema + invariants

Emit write evidence bundle

Commit or rollback atomically


What varies:


Registry type

Allowed patch ops

Validation schemas


Why this matters


Makes registry mutation first-class and auditable

Prevents “special” registry writes

Aligns with SSOT guarantees



5. Parallel Workstream Execution


Current state


Parallelism is specified, but execution safety relies on:


Human discipline

Tool-specific behavior

Post-hoc reconciliation


Convert into a reusable pattern


Pattern: Workstream Slice Executor


Invariant behavior:


Each workstream declares:
Scope

Write set

Output kind (deltas only)

Executor enforces:
No global writes

Scope boundaries

Deterministic merge order


Why this matters


Scales AI execution safely

Prevents cross-workstream corruption

Enables predictable multi-CLI execution



6. Evidence Collection & Bundling


Current state


Evidence exists everywhere, but:


Shapes differ

Naming varies

Consumers infer meaning


Convert into a reusable pattern


Pattern: Evidence Bundle Generator


Every step (gate, phase, write, merge) produces:


Standardized bundle:
summary.json

artifacts.json

logs/

checksums.json


Why this matters


Uniform audit surface

Tool-agnostic verification

Simplifies compliance and reporting



Bottom Line


You already proved the model with gates.


The next leverage points to convert into reusable pattern programs are:


Phase execution

Artifact handoff

Self-healing beyond gates

Registry mutation

Parallel workstreams

Evidence bundling


If a process:


Has a binary outcome

Consumes declared inputs

Produces declared outputs

Needs auditability


…it should be a reusable deterministic program, not bespoke logic.


If you want, the next concrete step is to define a “Reusable Program Taxonomy” (Gate, Phase, Mutation, Transport, Executor) and map every part of newPhasePlanProcess to exactly one category.

Thought for 28s


According to a document from 2026-02-03, newPhasePlanProcess v3.0.0 is built around step-level contracts, evidence chains, bounded self-healing, worktree isolation, ground-truth levels, and a gate system (19 gates + 14 FM checks)【


NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…


】【


NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…


】. Below is a Reusable Program Taxonomy and a one-to-one mapping of the major “parts” of the process into exactly one category.



Reusable Program Taxonomy


1) Gate


Definition: A binary validator run against declared inputs that emits a GateResult + evidence artifacts, optionally with a deterministic remediation loop (BG-DRL).
Signature: (inputs + commands + evidence_contract) → GateResult + evidence【


NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…


】【


Reusable gates and automation


】


2) Phase


Definition: A macro step in the plan’s phase graph: executes a set of steps (each with the required contract fields) and produces phase evidence and outputs.
Signature: phase_contract + steps[] → PhaseResult + phase outputs【


NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…


】


3) Mutation


Definition: Any authorized write to shared state (registries, SSOT files) performed via contract-driven/validated ingestion (not ad hoc editing).
Signature: mutation_contract → validated atomic update (+ evidence)【


REGISTRY_PLANNING_INTEGRATION_S…


】【


NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…


】


4) Transport


Definition: Deterministic handoff wrapper moving artifacts/evidence between phases/steps without implicit reads.
Signature: ArtifactEnvelope/HandoffReport → next phase input【


Reusable gates and automation


】


5) Executor


Definition: The runtime orchestration engines that (a) schedule/route, (b) run gates/phases/workstreams, (c) enforce isolation, timeouts, structured outputs, retries, and merge discipline.
Signature: plan + dependency/phase graph → executed run + reports【


NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…


】【


NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…


】



Map every part of newPhasePlanProcess to exactly one category


Gate


Validation gate catalog (19 gates) and their execution in dependency order (topological sort)【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】

Failure Mode checks (FM-01 through FM-14) executed after gates【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】

Ground truth validation levels (L0–L5) as validators (the system treats them as validation units with evidence requirements)【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】

CI compatibility validation gate (VAL-CI-COMPAT) (no TTY, structured outputs, timeouts, exit codes)【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】


Phase


Plan phases[] themselves (phase graph / ordered phases in the plan template)【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】

Step-level contract enforcement as a phase responsibility (steps must carry the required contract fields; v3 makes step contracts mandatory)【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】


Mutation


Registry ingestion pipeline that processes the planning “file mutation set” and applies validated create/update/delete actions to registry state【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】【
REGISTRY_PLANNING_INTEGRATION_S…
】

Central merge application of deltas/patches into shared registries/state (control checkout applies merges; workers never write global registries)【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】


Transport


Evidence path convention and evidence packaging by phase/step (evidence paths are standardized: .state/evidence/{phase_id}/{step_id}/)【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】

ArtifactEnvelope / HandoffReport style phase-to-phase handoff (canonical wrapper carrying identity, artifact refs, evidence refs, signatures, provenance)【
Reusable gates and automation
】


Executor


Plan CLI runtime (NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_plan_cli.py) as the orchestrator that runs gates and executes plans (including self-healing dispatch)【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】

Gate execution engine: loads scripts/gate_dependencies.json, executes gates, records results, aggregates validation report【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】

Parallel worktree / multi-workstream execution controller: workers run deltas-only; control merges + validates + rollback discipline【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】

Self-healing runtime: detection logic + bounded attempts + escalation implemented in the execution engine【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】

CI/CD pipeline runner integration: generate plan → run gates → execute plan → publish metrics (automation flow, constraints)【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】

Metrics/report generation step (e.g., generate_metrics_report.py consuming .state/metrics/metrics.jsonl)【
NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_S…
】



Notes on “exactly one category”


Anything that decides pass/fail is mapped to Gate, even if it runs inside a phase.

Anything that writes shared state is Mutation, even if triggered by execution.

Anything that moves artifacts/evidence between steps/phases is Transport.

Anything that routes/schedules/runs/merges/retries is Executor.

Anything that is a plan-defined stage of work is Phase.


This mapping is consistent with the “data-defined gates + shared runners” and “deterministic phase handoff” patterns described in the reusable patterns document【


Reusable gates and automation


】【


Reusable gates and automation


】.

Thought for 23s


The artifacts are the same “shape” for every gate. Per the reusable gate pattern, each gate is an instance of:


GateSpec → GateRun → GateResult → (optional) HealSequenceReport → Final Verdict, and the only gate-specific variability is what the evidence contract requires (kinds/paths/schema_ids).


Reusable gates and automation


Reusable gates and automation


The required artifact sets (per gate)


Set A — GateSpec package (inputs = “data, not code”)


GateSpec.json (or embedded in plan) containing:
gate_id, validator_id

inputs

commands[]

evidence_contract.required_artifacts[] (each with kind, path, schema_id)

pass_criteria (binary)

failure_fingerprint_contract
Reusable gates and automation
Reusable gates and automation


Set B — Required evidence bundle (gate-specific, but schema-bound)


All artifacts declared in evidence_contract.required_artifacts[], each validated against its schema_id.
Example shown: validator_report at evidence/gates/{{gate_id}}/validator_report.json with schema_id: schemas/validator_report.schema.json
Reusable gates and automation

This is the part that differs by gate: which evidence artifacts, and which schemas validate them.

Set C — GateResult (mandatory, stable output)


GateResult.json containing at minimum:
gate_id, validator_id

passed (true/false)

exit_code

normalized error_codes[]

failure_fingerprint (derived using the fingerprint contract)

evidence_paths[] pointing to the evidence bundle(s)
Reusable gates and automation
Reusable gates and automation


(Those fields are explicitly part of the normalization + result emission steps.)


Reusable gates and automation


Set D — Heal artifacts (conditional; only when passed=false)


HealSequenceReport.json (optional, but required when healing occurs)
indicates healed: true|false, what heal templates ran, and the outcome

followed by a rerun producing a final GateResult
Reusable gates and automation
Reusable gates and automation



Summary: “what must exist after every gate run”


Minimum deterministic closure for any gate is:


GateSpec (declares contract)

Evidence bundle(s) required by the contract, schema-validated

GateResult.json

+ HealSequenceReport.json if remediation was invoked
Reusable gates and automation


If you want, I can map these sets onto your actual gate catalog (e.g., “these 5 evidence artifacts belong to Gate X”), but that requires reading the gate list source that enumerates each gate’s evidence_contract.required_artifacts[].

Excluding the per-gate differences (inputs/commands/evidence_contract/schema_ids + normalization mapping), the intent of the reusable-pattern design is:


One shared runner implementation for all gates

Zero per-gate “runner scripts”

At most: small, bounded “gate adapters” (preferably data-only), not a separate script per gate【
Reusable gates and automation
】【】


What is reusable across all gates (one script/module each)


GateRunner (shared)
Acquire locks (if required)

Run commands[]

Collect raw outputs

Normalize outputs → exit_code, error_codes[], validator_id

Validate evidence schemas

Emit GateResult.json + fingerprint
This is explicitly described as “a shared GateRunner does the same steps for every gate.”【】

SelfHealOrchestrator (shared)
Map failure → failure_mode_id

Run ordered heal templates

Emit HealSequenceReport

Re-run gate and finalize
Also described as shared remediation logic, not per-gate healing scripts.【】

Fingerprinting + normalization engine (shared)
The fingerprint contract is standardized; only which fields are included is data-driven.【】


What is gate-specific (should NOT be separate “scripts per gate”)


Per the spec, gate-specific logic is confined to a small adapter surface:


Input shape (which files/ids/registries)

Command recipe

Evidence extractor mapping tool output → normalized error_codes[]

Evidence schemas used

Binary pass criteria【】


Direct answer


There should be 1 reusable “gate runner” script/module that runs every gate.
Each gate should not have its own runner script. Gates differ by configuration and adapter mapping, not by separate execution code paths.【】


If you see a design where every gate has its own “run_gate_X.py”, that’s a regression: it defeats decision elimination and makes determinism harder to enforce uniformly.

Thought for 9s


According to a document from 2026-02-03, the “reusable gates” approach is explicitly designed so everything is shared except the gate adapter inputs/commands/evidence extraction + evidence schema IDs (and the pass criteria remains binary).


Reusable gates and automation


Reusable gates and automation


Yes: you can formalize a reusable process for adding a new gate so it becomes a fully functioning template-integrated gate with deterministic outputs.


Reusable process to add a new gate (end-to-end)


0) Gate intake


Goal: prevent “special-case gates.”


Define the gate’s purpose in one sentence.

Confirm it can be expressed as a binary gate (pass/fail) with deterministic evidence. (If it can’t, it’s not a gate; it’s a phase/task.)


1) Create the GateSpec (data-only)


Create a GateSpec entry with only the allowed customization surface:


inputs

commands[]

evidence_contract (required artifacts + schema_id per artifact)

error_code_normalization mapping (tool output → normalized enums)

pass_criteria (still binary)
Reusable gates and automation


This is the “adapter,” and it is the only gate-specific part.


Reusable gates and automation


2) Define/extend evidence schemas (only when needed)


For every required_artifact in evidence_contract, ensure there is a schema ID that validates it (or define a new one).


The point is: evidence is not “files exist”; it’s “files validate.”
Reusable gates and automation


3) Register failure modes + heal sequences (data-only)


If the gate can fail in ways you want to remediate automatically:


Add new FailureMode objects

Add deterministic matcher conditions

Add ordered heal templates

No per-gate healing scripts (healing is shared orchestration + reusable templates).
Reusable gates and automation


4) Run with the shared GateRunner (no gate-specific runner script)


Execution is always the same:


Acquire locks (if required)

Run commands[]

Collect raw outputs

Normalize → error_codes[], exit_code, validator_id

Validate evidence schemas

Emit GateResult + failure_fingerprint
Reusable gates and automation


This is explicitly “a shared GateRunner does the same steps for every gate.”


Reusable gates and automation


5) Self-heal loop (shared) if failed


If passed=false:


classify failure mode

run heal templates

emit HealSequenceReport

rerun gate

escalate if still failing
Reusable gates and automation


6) Deterministic artifact closure for the gate


Every gate must produce the same outputs:


GateResult.json

optional HealSequenceReport.json

evidence bundle(s) referenced by the result
Reusable gates and automation


7) Template integration (what it means in practice)


To be “template-integrated,” the gate must be discoverable and runnable by the orchestrator as data, not hardcoded:


It appears in the gate catalog/list used by execution.

Its GateSpec points to its schemas and evidence contract.

Its outputs land in standard evidence paths and are schema-valid.


(That’s the enforcement mechanism that keeps gates uniform.)



Direct answer to your question about scripts


Yes — one reusable gate execution script/module should run all gates.
Each gate should not have its own runner script.


Per the spec:


Shared: GateRunner + SelfHealOrchestrator + shared schemas (GateResult, HealTemplateResult, HealSequenceReport, FailureMode).
Reusable gates and automation

Gate-specific: GateSpec (inputs/commands/evidence_contract/normalization/pass_criteria).
Reusable gates and automation


If you’re seeing separate run_<gate>.py per gate, that violates the model and will drift.



If you want the next concrete step, the document itself calls it out: define a single gate_spec.schema.json and gate_result.schema.json so the shared runner can consume/produce identical shapes for every gate and variability stays confined to the GateSpec data.


Reusable gates and automation
